title: Data Dictionary JSON (dd-json)
description: The canonical JSON representation of a data dictionary produced by dd_api's DataDictionary.to_json() and accepted by from_json(). A versioned wrapper around a list of data elements that mirror the typed model.

version.description: Schema version of this representation. Consumers should reject versions they do not understand.

elements.description: The data elements, in order (element order mirrors the order of fields in the datafile).

enumItem.description: One permissible value: the value as it appears in the datafile, a human-readable label, and an optional ontology term IRI.

dataElement.description: One field of the datafile. Blank single-valued fields are null; list-valued fields are arrays (possibly empty).

datatype.description: A datatype name from the specification (e.g. integer, string, date_mdy).

description.description: What the field means (may contain Markdown); null when absent.

section.description: The section (group of related fields) this element belongs to; null when the dictionary has no sections.

cardinality.description: Whether one datafile cell holds one value (single) or several (multiple).

terms.description: Ontology term identifiers (full IRIs or compact OBO ids).

pattern.description: An XSD-flavour regular expression values must match; null when none.

unit.description: The unit of measure as written; null when none.

enumeration.description: The permissible values, when the field is restricted to a fixed choice list; empty otherwise.

missing_value_codes.description: Codes that stand for 'no data' (refused, not collected, ...).

precondition.description: The condition under which the field holds a value (the Precondition grammar); null when the field always applies.

required.description: Whether the field must hold a value (subject to its precondition).

provenance.description: Where the field came from (study, instrument, URL); null when absent.

see_also.description: A URL with more information; null when absent.
